title: Phase 1: Bring the brain online and let Claude Code read it
summary: Stand up Graphiti + FalkorDB in compose, seed it from any Notion target via a generic one-shot migrator, and wire Claude Code in any client repo to query the brain via an injection hook + MCP.
outOfScope: Write-back from Claude Code sessions into the graph (Phase 3)
Backups and observability beyond minimal smoke (Phase 4)
Per-item migration tracking / resume semantics (no migration log in Phase 1; add only if incremental re-runs become a real pain point)
technicalConsiderations: Two compose services (falkordb, graphiti) plus a Caddy vhost (brain.{domain}); no second store. docker-compose.local.yml overlay exposes graphiti on 127.0.0.1:8000 for laptop dev

Migrator is domain-agnostic and stateless: --target <notion-id> --kind {database,page,auto}; the script never knows the shape of the data, Graphiti's per-write extraction handles routing/dedup. Re-running re-posts everything

LLM stack defaults to Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 (LLM) + Voyage voyage-3-lite (embedder) — both Anthropic-family, single ecosystem, Voyage free tier covers personal scale. Provider swaps live in compose/graphiti-config.yaml (set llm.provider + embedder.provider) with .env recipes for OpenAI direct, OpenRouter, local Ollama. Graphiti needs an embedder, so Anthropic-only is not viable without a separate embedder provider
Client wiring lives in a kit (templates/claude-code-client/) the user copies into any project repo: .mcp.json + inject_memory.py + UserPromptSubmit hook entry
Bearer token + env vars BRAIN_DOMAIN and BRAIN_BEARER_TOKEN required in shell before launching claude; locally the bearer is unused since Caddy is VPS-only
Risks: extraction-LLM cost during bulk migration, MCP transport flakiness (REST fallback exists), entity dedup tuning
